  2. Main Content area shows Agentic Logo and Greeting text.
  3. Bottom Input area contains Text box, '+' (Attachment) button, and Send button.
  4. 'Build your own skills' button/link is visible.

Section: Agentic > UI
Title: Verify Landing Page - 'Build Your Own Skills' Card
Preconditions: User is on the new chat landing page
Steps: 1. Observe the 'Looking for a tailored solution?' section
Expected Result: 1. Card contains title, description, and 'Build Your Own Skills ->' button.

Section: Agentic > UI > Loading
Title: Verify Loading Animation (Cat Icon) while Agentic is preparing response
Preconditions: User is in a chat
Steps:
1. Type any message
2. Click Send
3. Observe the loading state
Expected Result:
1. Cat icon animation is displayed while Agentic is preparing the response.
2. Animation continues until response is ready.

Section: Agentic > UI > Loading > Edge Case
Title: Verify Loading Animation stops when user cancels request
Preconditions: User is in a chat with loading state
Steps:
1. Type a message and Click Send
2. While loading animation is showing, navigate away or refresh page
Expected Result:
1. Loading animation stops.
2. No hanging loading state on page reload.

Section: Agentic > UI > Loading > Edge Case
Title: Verify Multiple Messages Loading - Only latest shows loading
Preconditions: User sends messages rapidly
Steps:
1. Type and send message 1
2. Immediately type and send message 2
3. Observe loading state
Expected Result:
1. Only the latest message shows loading animation.
2. Previous messages do not show multiple loading indicators.

Section: Agentic > UI > Loading > Error Handling

Preconditions: User is in a chat

2. Click Send

Expected Result:
1. Error message 'Something went wrong. Please try again later.' is displayed.
2. Cat icon stops loading animation.

5. Click Send button
6. Wait for 15 seconds

System stops waiting at 15s
Error message displayed
Request aborted
No 60s timeout triggered

Section: Agentic > UI > Streaming State > Large Data
Title: Verify User Can use page responsively with large data response
Test Data: Prompt: ค้นหาข่าวเด่นทั้งหมดวันนี้
Preconditions: User is logged in on eko-dev.ekoapp.com and has an active chat
Steps:
1. User is in the Chat page
2. Type 'ค้นหาข่าวเด่นทั้งหมดวันนี้' in the input field
3. Click the Send button
4. While the agent is processing, attempt to scroll the page up and down
5. Attempt to click on the sidebar menu items
6. Attempt to type in the input field again
Expected Result:
1. The page remains responsive during agent processing.
2. Scrolling works smoothly without lag or jank.
3. Sidebar items are clickable and respond to hover/click.
4. The input field accepts new text input.
5. No browser 'Page Unresponsive' dialog appears.

Section: Agentic > UI > Streaming State > Large Data

Preconditions: User is logged in and has multiple existing chats in the sidebar

Expected Result:

2. The selected chat loads with its full history.
3. No white screen, hang, or unresponsive period occurs.
4. Returning to the original chat shows the agent's completed or in-progress response.

Section: Agentic > Base Skill > Assistants Integration
Title: Verify Agentic can call Assistants to answer questions from KM
Test Data: User has Assistants with KM data (e.g. village regulations)
Preconditions: User is in a chat with available Assistants
Steps:
1. Type a question related to data in Assistants' KM (e.g., 'พื้นที่ส่วนกลาง ตามนิยามในเอกสารหมายถึงอะไร')
2. Click Send
Expected Result:
1. Agentic calls the relevant Assistant.
2. Agentic uses KM data from Assistant to provide the answer.
3. Answer is displayed with correct information from KM.

Section: Agentic > Base Skill > Assistants Integration > UI
Title: Verify UI when Agentic calls Assistants and uses KM
Preconditions: Agentic has called an Assistant with KM
Steps: 1. Observe the response after Agentic uses KM from Assistant
Expected Result:
1. 'KM Agents' section is visible above the response.
  2. 'Expand All' link is available next to KM Agents label.
  3. Response shows 'คำตอบ:' section with information from KM.

Section: Agentic > Base Skill > Assistants Integration > UI
Title: Verify Expand functionality for KM Agents section
Preconditions: Agentic has called an Assistant with KM
Steps:
1. Click 'Expand All' next to KM Agents
2. Observe the expanded content
Expected Result:
1. 'Used Knowledge Bases' section is displayed.
2. Context details are shown including: Topic, Sub Topic, Sorting Score.
3. Content from KM is visible with full details.

Section: Agentic > Base Skill > Assistants Integration > UI
Title: Verify KM Agents shows Fulfillments reference
Preconditions: Agentic answered using KM data
Steps: 1. Observe the KM Agents response section
Expected Result:
1. 'อ้างอิงจาก Fulfillments:' section is visible.
2. Reference shows source document path (e.g., 'ระเบียบหมู่บ้านลลิลทาวน์ ไลโอ ลำลูกกา คลอง4-5 หมวดที่ 1, ข้อ 3').

Section: Agentic > Base Skill > Assistants Integration > Negative
Title: Verify behavior when no Assistants with relevant KM available
Preconditions: User asks question with no matching KM
Steps:
1. Type a question that no Assistant has KM for
2. Click Send
Expected Result:
1. Agentic responds without KM Agents section.
2. Response uses general knowledge or web search instead.

Section: Agentic > Base Skill > Assistants Integration > Negative
Title: Verify behavior when Assistants KM data is incomplete
Preconditions: Assistants has partial or corrupted KM
Steps:
1. Ask a question where KM data is incomplete
2. Click Send
Expected Result:
1. Agentic handles incomplete data gracefully.
2. Response indicates data limitation or asks for clarification.

Section: Agentic > Base Skill > Assistants Integration > Edge Case
Title: Verify Agentic chooses correct Assistant when multiple have similar KM
Preconditions: Multiple Assistants have related KM topics
Steps:
1. Type a question that could match multiple Assistants
2. Click Send
Expected Result:
1. Agentic selects the most relevant Assistant.
2. Response shows which Assistant/KM was used.
3. Answer is accurate and complete.

Section: Agentic > Base Skill > Assistants Integration > Edge Case
Title: Verify Collapsed state of KM Agents section by default
Preconditions: Agentic answered using KM
Steps: 1. Observe KM Agents section immediately after response
Expected Result:
1. KM Agents section is collapsed by default.
2. 'Expand All' text is visible indicating expandable content.

Section: Agentic > Base Skill > Assistants Integration > Edge Case
Title: Verify KM Agents section can be collapsed after expanding
Preconditions: KM Agents section is expanded
Steps:
1. Click 'Expand All' to expand
2. Click again to collapse
Expected Result:
1. Section collapses back to minimal view.
2. Toggle works correctly between states.

Section: Agentic > Base Skill > Assistants Integration > Edge Case
Title: Verify Sorting Score display in expanded KM view
Preconditions: KM section is expanded
Steps:
1. Expand KM Agents section
2. Observe Sorting Score value
Expected Result:
1. Sorting Score is displayed (e.g., 0.3240).

Section: Agentic > Base Skill > Internal Library > Language
Title: Verify Mity responds in Thai when user sends a Thai message using Internal Library
Test Data:
- Message : สวัสดี ช่วยหาข้อมูลให้หน่อย
- Internal Library : Enabled
Preconditions: User is logged in and has access to Agentic AI chat
Steps:
1. User is in Agentic AI chat page
2. Click '+ New Chat' to start a new conversation
3. Click '+ INTERNAL LIBRARY' button at the bottom of the chat input
4. Select an Internal Library tool
5. Type a message in Thai language in the chat input (e.g. 'สวัสดี ช่วยหาข้อมูลให้หน่อย')
6. Click the send button or press Enter
7. Observe the language of Mity's response
Expected Result: Mity responds in Thai language, matching the language the user sent the message in

Section: Agentic > Base Skill > Internal Library > Language
Title: Verify Mity responds in English when user sends an English message using Internal Library
Test Data:
- Message : Hello, please find information for me
- Internal Library : Enabled
Preconditions: User is logged in and has access to Agentic AI chat
Steps:
1. User is in Agentic AI chat page
2. Click '+ New Chat' to start a new conversation
3. Click '+ INTERNAL LIBRARY' button at the bottom of the chat input
4. Select an Internal Library tool
5. Type a message in English in the chat input (e.g. 'Hello, please find information for me')
6. Click the send button or press Enter
7. Observe the language of Mity's response
Expected Result: Mity responds in English language, matching the language the user sent the message in

Section: Agentic > Base Skill > Internal Library > Language
Title: Verify Mity responds in Thai when user switches from English to Thai mid-conversation using Internal Library
Test Data:
- First Message : Hello
- Second Message : ช่วยสรุปข้อมูลเป็นภาษาไทย
- Internal Library : Enabled
Preconditions: User is logged in and has an active Agentic AI chat with Internal Library enabled
Steps:
1. User is in Agentic AI chat page
2. Click '+ New Chat' to start a new conversation
3. Click '+ INTERNAL LIBRARY' button at the bottom of the chat input
4. Select an Internal Library tool
5. Type a message in English (e.g. 'Hello') and send
6. Wait for Mity's response
7. Type a follow-up message in Thai (e.g. 'ช่วยสรุปข้อมูลเป็นภาษาไทย') and send
8. Observe the language of Mity's second response
Expected Result: Mity responds in Thai for the second message, matching the language of the most recent user message

Section: Agentic > Base Skill > Internal Library > Language
Title: Verify Mity responds in the correct language when using different Internal Library tools
Test Data:
- Message : ช่วยค้นหาข้อมูลสินค้า
- Internal Library Tools : Multiple tools
Preconditions: User is logged in and has access to Agentic AI chat with multiple Internal Library tools available
Steps:
1. User is in Agentic AI chat page
2. Click '+ New Chat' to start a new conversation
3. Click '+ INTERNAL LIBRARY' button at the bottom of the chat input

8. Click '+ INTERNAL LIBRARY' and select a different Internal Library tool
9. Type a message in Thai and send
10. Observe the language of Mity's response
Expected Result: Mity responds in Thai for both tools, confirming language matching works across all Internal Library tools

Section: Agentic > Base Skill > Internal Library > Knowledge Management
Title: Verify that deleting a file in ASAP removes it from both ASAP and Library
Preconditions: User is logged in on Web. User has an existing file in ASAP and Library.

1. Login to the account
2. Navigate to the Knowledge (ASAP) page on the sidebar
3. Click 'Setting' on Knowledge
4. Locate the file 'ASAP_knowledge_management.png'
5. Click the delete icon
6. Click the delete button on the confirmation dialog
7. Check the file list in ASAP
8. Navigate to the Library page on the sidebar
9. Check the file list in Library
Expected Result: The confirmation dialog appears and closes successfully. The file is removed from the ASAP platform. The file is also removed from the Library platform.

Section: Agentic > Base Skill > Internal Library > Knowledge Management

Preconditions: User is logged in on Web. User has copied the direct URL of an existing file.

Section: Agentic > Base Skill > Internal Library > Context Retention
Title: Verify system uses previous context when user asks follow-up question
Test Data: Data : Active User with library access
Preconditions: User logged in. Internal library mode activated. At least one previous message sent about a specific topic.
Steps:
1. Login to the account
2. Navigate to Chat
3. Click (+) on chat field
4. Click internal library button
5. Ask a question about a specific internal topic (e.g., "What is the company leave policy?") and press enter
6. Wait for the response
7. Ask a follow-up question referencing the previous context (e.g., "How many days?") and press enter
8. Verify the AI response
Expected Result: System uses the context from the previous message and responds with a relevant answer that continues the conversation thread

Section: Agentic > Base Skill > Internal Library > Context Retention
Title: Verify system maintains context across multiple consecutive messages
Test Data: Data : Active User with library access
Preconditions: User logged in. Internal library mode activated.
Steps:
1. Login to the account
2. Navigate to Chat
3. Click (+) on chat field
4. Click internal library button
5. Ask a question about an internal topic and press enter
6. Wait for the response
7. Ask a second follow-up question and press enter
8. Wait for the response
9. Ask a third follow-up question referencing details from both prior responses and press enter
10. Verify the AI response
Expected Result: System maintains conversation context across all three messages and provides a coherent response that incorporates information from all previous exchanges

4. Wait for the error message to appear
5. Turn Wi-Fi or Mobile Data back on
6. Wait 5 seconds for reconnection
7. Type a new message and tap Send
Expected Result:
"1. The error_timeout message was shown during the offline period.
2. After reconnecting, the new message is sent successfully.
3. The agent responds normally to the new message.
4. Previous error_timeout message remains in chat history."

Section: Agentic > iOS > Chat > Error Handling
Title: Verify User Can see app stability with consecutive timeout errors
Preconditions: User is logged in and on an active chat screen
Steps:
1. User is in active chat screen
2. Type <<TIMEOUT_TESTING>> and tap Send
3. Wait for the error message to appear
4. Repeat typing <<TIMEOUT_TESTING>> and sending four more times
5. Observe app behavior throughout
Expected Result:
"1. Each '<<TIMEOUT_TESTING>>' produces a corresponding error_timeout message.
2. All 5 error messages are displayed in chronological order in the chat.
3. The app does not crash, freeze, or become unresponsive.
4. Scrolling remains smooth through the error messages."

Section: Agentic > iOS > Chat > Error Handling

6. Navigate to the same chat conversation
Expected Result:
"1. The previous error_timeout message is visible in the chat history.
2. The translated error text is displayed correctly (not blank or corrupted).
3. Chat history order is preserved."

Section: Agentic > iOS > Chat > Error Handling
Title: Verify User Can see timeout error in history with chat switching
Preconditions: User has at least two chat conversations

6. Switch back to Chat A via sidebar
Expected Result:
"1. Chat A still shows the error_timeout message.
2. The error text is displayed correctly.
3. No data loss or duplication of messages occurs."

Section: Agentic > iOS > Chat > Error Handling
Title: Verify User Can not see retry button with timeout error message

2. If a retry button exists: it is clearly labeled and tappable.
3. If no retry button exists: the user can only retry by sending a new message manually.
4. Document the observed behavior for acceptance criteria confirmation."

Section: Agentic > iOS > Chat > Error Handling
Title: Verify User Can see case-sensitive error with <<TIMEOUT_TESTING>> keyword variations

Section: Agentic > Android > Chat > Error Handling
Title: Verify User Can see timeout error message with <<TIMEOUT_TESTING>> keyword
Preconditions: User is logged in and on an active chat screen with internet connected
Steps:
1. User is in active chat screen
  2. Type <>
  3. Tap Send icon
  4. Observe the agent response area
Expected Result:
"1. The agent responds with a message bubble containing the error_timeout status.

  1. The error message text reads 'ไม่พบการตอบกลับจากเซิร์ฟเวอร์ กรุณาลองใหม่อีกครั้งในภายหลัง' (or the equivalent client-translated text for the user's language).
  2. The agent avatar (cat ears icon) is displayed alongside the error message.
  3. The message appears in the correct chat position (left-aligned as an agent response)."

Section: Agentic > Android > Chat > Error Handling
Title: Verify User Can see timeout error message with disconnected internet
Preconditions: User is logged in and on an active chat screen with internet connected

3. Turn off Wi-Fi and Mobile Data
4. Type a new message in the chat input field
5. Tap Send icon
6. Observe the chat area for the error response
Expected Result:
"1. The message is sent or queued from the user side.
2. An error_timeout message is displayed with the translated text 'ไม่พบการตอบกลับจากเซิร์ฟเวอร์ กรุณาลองใหม่อีกครั้งในภายหลัง'.
3. The error message clearly indicates a server timeout, not a generic error.
4. The agent avatar is displayed alongside the error message."

Section: Agentic > Android > Chat > Error Handling
Title: Verify User Can use chat functionally with internet reconnection
Preconditions: User is on an active chat with internet connected
Steps:
1. User is in active chat screen
2. Turn off Wi-Fi and Mobile Data
3. Type a message and tap Send
4. Wait for the error message to appear
5. Turn Wi-Fi or Mobile Data back on
6. Wait 5 seconds for reconnection
7. Type a new message and tap Send
Expected Result:
"1. The error_timeout message was shown during the offline period.
2. After reconnecting, the new message is sent successfully.
3. The agent responds normally to the new message.
4. Previous error_timeout message remains in chat history."

Section: Agentic > Android > Chat > Error Handling

2. All 5 error messages are displayed in chronological order in the chat.
3. The app does not crash, freeze, or become unresponsive.
4. Scrolling remains smooth through the error messages."

Section: Agentic > Android > Chat > MCP Loading Indicator
Title: Verify that the loading state is displayed when the agent is processing a request
Preconditions: User is logged in on the Android app. User is in a chat with an Agent.
Steps:
1. Login to the account
  2. Navigate to the chat page with an Agent
  3. Send a message to the agent (e.g., 'Hello, what can you do?')
  4. Observe the UI immediately after sending the message while waiting for a response
Expected Result: A visible loading indicator (e.g., spinner or skeleton text) is displayed while the agent is processing the request.

Section: Agentic > Android > Chat > MCP Loading Indicator
Title: Verify that the 'Thinking' state is displayed and conveys progress
Preconditions: User is logged in on the Android app. User is in a chat with an Agent.
Steps:
1. Login to the account
2. Navigate to the chat page with an Agent
3. Send a complex request that requires the agent to use tools or think
4. Observe the agent's message bubble before the final answer is provided
Expected Result: A 'Thinking...' indicator or expandable thinking steps view is displayed, correctly indicating that the agent is actively working on the request.

Section: Agentic > Android > Chat > MCP Loading Indicator
Title: Verify that the user instructions or prompts are accurately displayed
Preconditions: User is logged in on the Android app. User is in a chat with an Agent.
Steps:
1. Login to the account
2. Navigate to the chat page with an Agent
3. Type a multi-line or detailed instruction in the input field
4. Send the message
5. Verify how the sent instruction appears in the chat transcript
Expected Result: The sent instruction is displayed accurately without text truncation, layout breaks, or formatting issues.

Section: Agentic > Android > Chat > MCP Loading Indicator
Title: Verify that the loading and thinking states are hidden once the final response arrives
Preconditions: User is logged in on the Android app. User is in a chat with an Agent.
Steps:
1. Login to the account
2. Navigate to the chat page with an Agent
3. Send a message to the agent
4. Wait for the final, complete response to appear
Expected Result: The loading spinner and active 'Thinking...' state disappear. Only the final response (and a collapsed thinking steps block if applicable) remains visible.

Section: Agentic > Android > Chat > MCP Loading Indicator
Title: Verify the UI behavior when network connection is lost during the loading or thinking state
Preconditions: User is logged in on the Android app. User is in a chat with an Agent.
Steps:
1. Login to the account
  2. Navigate to the chat page with an Agent
  3. Send a message to the agent
  4. While the agent is in the loading or thinking state, disconnect the network (e.g., turn on Airplane Mode)
  5. Observe the UI behavior
Expected Result: The application handles the network loss gracefully, displaying an appropriate error message (e.g., 'Network disconnected' or 'Failed to send message') and does not freeze indefinitely.

Section: Agentic > SharePoint KM > ASAP Integration > Managed Resource Creation
Title: Check that setting sharepointConfig.enabled=true in network-config should auto-create managed KM and SYSTEM_SHAREPOINT_KM assistant
Preconditions: Valid networkId exists / Admin API key configured / No existing SharePoint managed KM for this network
Steps:
1. Send PATCH network-config with sharepointConfig.enabled=true for net-001
2. Query the knowledge table for networkId=net-001 and managed=true and source='sharepoint'
3. Query the assistants table for SYSTEM_SHAREPOINT_KM linked to net-001
4. Verify fulfillment record is created
Expected Result:
1. knowledge table contains a managed=true / source='sharepoint' record for net-001
2. SYSTEM_SHAREPOINT_KM assistant is created and linked to net-001
3. Fulfillment record is present

Section: Agentic > SharePoint KM > ASAP Integration > Managed Resource Creation
Title: Verify that enabling sharepointConfig on a second network should create independent managed KM without affecting the first network
Test Data: networkId: net-001 (already enabled) / networkId: net-002 (new enable)
Preconditions: net-001 already has SharePoint managed KM / net-002 exists / Admin API key configured
Steps:
1. Send PATCH network-config with sharepointConfig.enabled=true for net-002
2. Query knowledge table for net-001 managed SharePoint records
3. Query knowledge table for net-002 managed SharePoint records
4. Verify unique constraint (networkId / managed / source) holds for both
Expected Result:
1. net-002 has its own managed KM and assistant created
2. net-001 records are unchanged
3. Unique constraint is satisfied for both networks

Section: Agentic > SharePoint KM > ASAP Integration > Managed Resource Creation
Title: Verify that disabling sharepointConfig.enabled should NOT delete the managed KM or SYSTEM_SHAREPOINT_KM assistant
Preconditions: net-001 has existing SharePoint managed KM and assistant / Admin API key configured
Steps:
1. Send PATCH network-config with sharepointConfig.enabled=false for net-001
2. Query knowledge table for net-001 managed SharePoint records
3. Query assistants table for SYSTEM_SHAREPOINT_KM linked to net-001
Expected Result:
1. Managed KM record for net-001 still exists in knowledge table
2. SYSTEM_SHAREPOINT_KM assistant record still exists
3. No teardown or deletion is triggered

Section: Agentic > SharePoint KM > ASAP Integration > Managed Resource Creation
Title: Check that attempting to create a second managed SharePoint KM for the same network should be rejected by unique constraint
Preconditions: net-001 already has managed SharePoint KM / DB access to knowledge table
Steps:
1. Attempt to directly insert a second managed=true / source='sharepoint' record for net-001 into knowledge table
2. Observe database response
3. Verify existing record is unchanged
Expected Result:
1. Insert is rejected with a unique constraint violation error
2. Only one managed SharePoint KM record exists for net-001

Section: Agentic > SharePoint KM > ASAP Integration > Managed Resource Creation
Title: Check that enabling sharepointConfig with an invalid or nonexistent networkId should return an error and not create orphaned records
Preconditions: Admin API key configured / networkId net-999 does not exist
Steps:
1. Send PATCH network-config with sharepointConfig.enabled=true for net-999
2. Check knowledge table for any orphaned records with networkId=net-999
3. Check assistants table
Expected Result:
1. API returns 404 or 400 error
2. No KM records created in knowledge table
3. No assistant records created

Section: Agentic > SharePoint KM > ASAP Integration > Managed Resource Creation
Title: Verify that enabling sharepointConfig when a non-SharePoint managed KM already exists for the network should succeed without violating unique constraint
Test Data: Existing managed KM: networkId=net-001 / managed=true / source='eko' / New: source='sharepoint'
Preconditions: net-001 has managed=true KM with source='eko' / Admin API key configured
Steps:
1. Verify existing managed KM source='eko' record for net-001
2. Send PATCH network-config with sharepointConfig.enabled=true for net-001
3. Query knowledge table for net-001
Expected Result:
1. SharePoint managed KM is created successfully (source='sharepoint')
2. Existing source='eko' managed KM is unaffected
3. Unique constraint (networkId / managed / source) is satisfied for both records

Section: Agentic > SharePoint KM > ASAP Integration > Managed Resource Creation
Title: Check that re-enabling sharepointConfig after it was previously disabled should NOT create a duplicate managed KM record
Test Data: network-config flow: enabled=true → enabled=false → enabled=true / networkId: net-001
Preconditions: net-001 previously had SharePoint enabled then disabled / managed KM record still present
Steps:
1. Confirm existing managed SharePoint KM record for net-001
2. Send PATCH network-config with sharepointConfig.enabled=true for net-001 again
3. Count managed SharePoint KM records for net-001 in knowledge table
Expected Result:
1. Exactly one managed SharePoint KM record exists for net-001
2. No duplicate record created
3. Unique constraint not violated

Section: Agentic > SharePoint KM > ASAP Integration > ETL Content Sync

2. Wait for next ETL sync cycle (or trigger manually)

4. Verify document content is searchable
Expected Result:
1. report.pdf is indexed in Google Discovery Engine
2. Document is retrievable via search query
3. ETL sync log shows successful ingestion

Section: Agentic > SharePoint KM > ASAP Integration > ETL Content Sync

Expected Result:

Section: Agentic > SharePoint KM > ASAP Integration > ETL Content Sync

Section: Agentic > SharePoint KM > ASAP Integration > Delta Sync
Title: Check that delta sync should only ingest changed items since the last sync using Graph API delta query

Steps:

2. Modify update.docx in SharePoint
3. Trigger delta sync cycle
4. Inspect ETL logs for files processed
5. Verify only update.docx was re-indexed
Expected Result:

2. Unchanged files are NOT re-downloaded or re-indexed
3. Delta token is updated in Cosmos after successful sync

Section: Agentic > SharePoint KM > ASAP Integration > Delta Sync

2. Trigger a delta sync with one changed file
3. After sync completes query Cosmos for net-001 deltaToken
4. Compare old and new token values
Expected Result:
1. deltaToken value in Cosmos is updated after sync
2. New token differs from old token
3. Next delta sync uses the new token

Section: Agentic > SharePoint KM > ASAP Integration > Delta Sync
Title: Verify that a deleted file in SharePoint should be excluded from Discovery Engine search results after delta sync

Steps:

2. Delete obsolete.pdf from SharePoint
3. Trigger delta sync
4. Search Discovery Engine for content unique to obsolete.pdf
Expected Result:
